2022-03-01.title: A few members of the Russian Parliament speak out against the war.
2022-03-01.abstract: In a rare show of dissent, three deputies of the State Duma have expressed regret about the attack.

2022-03-01.title: The U.S. expels 12 Russian diplomats for spying as Ukraine dominates action at the U.N.
2022-03-01.abstract: The move is part of a larger effort to isolate Russia diplomatically and economically since it invaded Ukraine last week.

2022-03-01.title: Chinese Company Removed as Operator of Cobalt Mine in Congo
2022-03-01.abstract: A court has given the Congolese control of one of the world’s largest sources of cobalt while allegations against the mine’s Chinese owners are investigated.

2022-03-01.title: Satellite internet terminals, sent by Elon Musk, arrive in Ukraine.
2022-03-01.abstract: Responding to a plea by a Ukrainian official, Mr. Musk delivered an unknown number of his Starlink terminals, which offer online connection via orbiting satellites, bypassing conventional providers.

2022-03-01.title: The Pfizer vaccine is less effective in 5- to 11-year-olds than in older groups, new data show.
2022-03-01.abstract: The coronavirus vaccine made by Pfizer-BioNTech is much less effective in preventing infection in children ages 5 to 11 years than in older adolescents or adults, according to a large new set of data collected by health officials in New York State — a finding that has significant ramifications for these children and their parents.

2022-03-02.title: Spurning Rebukes, Russia Escalates Attack on Ukraine as Refugee Ranks Swell
2022-03-02.abstract: There was no sign of possible de-escalation on the sixth day of the invasion, as Russia appeared determined to toughen its tactics against a defiant neighbor.

2022-03-02.title: ‘There Will Be a Battle’: A Family Prepares for War in Kyiv
2022-03-02.abstract: As the war in Ukraine moves closer to the capital, some families are facing a difficult choice: Stay home or take up arms.

2022-03-02.title: An Indian student was killed while getting food for others sheltering in Kharkiv.
2022-03-02.abstract: Naveen Shekharappa was killed when he left a bunker in Kharkiv to fetch food, officials and his family members said.

2022-03-02.title: Explosive Scenes as New Zealand Police Move to End Weekslong Occupation
2022-03-02.abstract: Fires and violence at a protest opposing vaccine mandates were a rare sight in a country known for its relative serenity and stability.

2022-03-02.title: ‘We Are on Our Side’: Across Asia, a Mixed Reaction to Ukraine War
2022-03-02.abstract: While most American allies in the region have fallen in line, authoritarian governments and those with weaker ties to the West have been more reluctant to act.

2022-03-03.title: Philadelphia drops its mask mandate.
2022-03-03.abstract: As the Omicron wave continues to subside, a growing number of cities and states are relaxing their pandemic restrictions.

2022-03-03.title: One week into the war and with cities under siege, a humanitarian crisis looms.
2022-03-03.abstract: One day after Russian forces seized the first major city in their onslaught in Ukraine — potentially clearing the way for a bid to seize the entire Black Sea coast and cut off the country from world shipping — Russian forces on Thursday continued to lay siege to major cities, creating a dire humanitarian crisis.

2022-03-03.title: As Cases Skyrocket, New Zealand Finally Faces Its Covid Reckoning
2022-03-03.abstract: New Zealanders are anxious as they learn to live with the pandemic-related risk that has been a fact of life elsewhere for two years.

2022-03-03.title: Hundreds Held After New Zealand-Led Investigation Into Images of Child Abuse
2022-03-03.abstract: An international collaboration resulted in the rescue of dozens of children and exposed a secret global network used to share material on sexual abuse.

2022-03-03.title: In Glitter and Leotards, They Took a Stand: Carnival Must Go On
2022-03-03.abstract: After Brazil’s biggest party was effectively canceled for the second straight year, a group of artists rallied to play on despite police orders.

2022-03-04.title: Videos show scenes from one occupied Ukrainian city.
2022-03-04.abstract: ​​Videos verified by The New York Times show some residents of Melitopol meeting Russian troops with a mix of anger and scorn, and others lining up for aid in a central square.

2022-03-04.title: A fire breaks out at a nuclear plant during a Russian assault, Ukraine says.
2022-03-04.abstract: Security camera footage showed a building ablaze inside the complex amid fighting. The fire was later extinguished. A Ukrainian agency said that Russian forces had taken control of the site.

2022-03-04.title: What to Read About the Russia-Ukraine War
2022-03-04.abstract: Our reporters have been covering the crisis, from documenting military movement and the refugee exodus on the ground to analyzing global geopolitical developments.

2022-03-04.title: Catch up on the latest news on Ukraine.
2022-03-04.abstract: Russian troops in southeastern Ukraine have seized Europe’s largest nuclear power plant, Ukrainian officials said on Friday, but a fire there that had raised worldwide alarms was extinguished. There had been worries that the fire could spread to nuclear reactors and lead to radiation leaks, but international monitors said Friday morning that there was no immediate sign that radiation had leaked during the battle for the plant.

2022-03-04.title: In a Run-Down Roman Villa, a Princess From Texas Awaits Her Next Act
2022-03-04.abstract: Princess Rita Boncompagni Ludovisi, formerly Rita Jenrette, is locked in a battle over the estate of her late husband, a Roman prince.

2022-03-05.title: Russians Seize Europe’s Biggest Nuclear Plant and Gain in the South as More Ukrainians Flee
2022-03-05.abstract: The Russian military advance in Ukraine sowed more destruction and chaos, pointing to a worsening conflict, as the Kremlin also moved to restrict how it’s reported at home.

2022-03-05.title: Zelensky criticizes NATO over its rejection of a no-fly zone.
2022-03-05.abstract: “All the people who will die from this day will die because of you, as well,” the Ukrainian president told NATO leaders in a video shared on social media.

2022-03-05.title: North Korea Launches Ballistic Missile, South Korea Says
2022-03-05.abstract: The launch is North Korea’s second missile test in a week and comes only days before the presidential election in South Korea.

2022-03-05.title: China Outlines Plan to Stabilize Economy in Crucial Year for Xi
2022-03-05.abstract: China calls for heavy government spending and lending, as its leaders seek to project confidence in the face of global uncertainty over the pandemic and war in Ukraine.

2022-03-05.title: ‘The time has come’: Gov. Murphy of New Jersey ends his daily virus briefings.
2022-03-05.abstract: Friday’s briefing was two years to the day since the state’s first confirmed coronavirus case, the governor said, and served as a fitting end to his pandemic updates.

2022-03-06.title: Russian Prisoners and Ukrainian Soldiers Describe Two Sides of the Conflict
2022-03-06.abstract: The accounts painted a picture of a conflict consisting largely of ambushes, at least in northern Ukraine where the Russian forces are striving to encircle and capture the capital, Kyiv.

2022-03-06.title: Mexico Soccer Riot Leaves 26 Injured, Officials Say
2022-03-06.abstract: Videos posted on social media showed violent clashes between fans in a stadium in the central city of Querétaro on Saturday.

2022-03-06.title: Here is the latest news about Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.
2022-03-06.abstract: LVIV, Ukraine — After Western nations rejected his appeal to impose a no-fly zone over the country, President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine on Sunday called on the entire nation to resist the Russian invasion. He cheered the courage of protesters who filled the streets of occupied cities and towns, saying that “every meter of our Ukrainian land won by protest and humiliation of the invaders is a step forward, a step toward victory.”

2022-03-06.title: Here’s why Western leaders are reluctant to impose a no-fly zone over Ukraine.
2022-03-06.abstract: Enforcing a ban on aircraft could draw more countries into conflict against Russia and set off “a full-fledged war in Europe,” NATO’s leader has warned.

2022-03-06.title: In Burundi, the Drum Is a Revered Symbol of Unity. But Only Men Can Play.
2022-03-06.abstract: Burundian drumming is a global phenomenon, featured in blockbuster movies and best-selling albums. But at home, authorities have restricted it to official functions — and banned women from performing.

2022-03-07.title: Proud Band of Ukrainian Troops Holds Russian Assault at Bay — for Now
2022-03-07.abstract: “Few expected such strength from our people,” said a Ukrainian colonel whose soldiers have repelled a Russian attack on the port city of Mykolaiv for three days.

2022-03-07.title: Russia Keeps Up Attack, Again Halting Evacuation of Ukrainian Port City
2022-03-07.abstract: An encircled Mariupol faced intense shelling, while Russian forces kept up their slow advance on Ukraine’s capital, Kyiv.

2022-03-07.title: Italian City Tied to Russia by a Revered Saint Feels the Sting of War in Ukraine
2022-03-07.abstract: The port of Bari holds relics venerated by Orthodox Christians throughout the former Soviet bloc. Today it is also home to a spillover of tensions from Russia’s invasion.

2022-03-07.title: ‘No Wavering’: After Turning to Putin, Xi Faces Hard Wartime Choices for China
2022-03-07.abstract: Beijing aligned itself with Putin as Russia prepared to invade Ukraine. Now its efforts to edge away are constrained by leadership politics.

2022-03-07.title: ‘The Worst is Behind You’: Ukrainian Family Reunites in Poland
2022-03-07.abstract: Polish resident Ksenia Kraft reunited with her cousins and their five children near the border with Ukraine. The families fled the town of Berdychiv in central Ukraine after Russia’s invasion.

2022-03-08.title: Zelensky addressed Ukraine from his office, showing that he remained in Kyiv.
2022-03-08.abstract: The Ukrainian president told his people that he was not afraid or in hiding.

2022-03-08.title: Moderna opens the door to enforcing its vaccine patents in some countries.
2022-03-08.abstract: The company had previously pledged not to enforce its Covid vaccine patents anywhere during the pandemic.

2022-03-08.title: Venezuela could be a fill-in for Russian oil, but critics fear aiding another strongman.
2022-03-08.abstract: U.S. officials are reportedly considering easing sanctions on Venezuelan crude as oil prices have skyrocketed.

2022-03-08.title: A 459-Foot Mystery in a Tuscan Port: Is It a Russian’s Superyacht?
2022-03-08.abstract: As European authorities go after the luxury assets of oligarchs close to Vladimir Putin, a superyacht cloaked in secrecy has come under investigation.

2022-03-08.title: For this border crisis, Poles extend a warm welcome, unlike last time.
2022-03-08.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of Polish families have opened their homes to refugees from Ukraine, a sharp contrast to the country’s response to a different crisis last fall at the border with Belarus.

2022-03-09.title: Quoting Churchill and Shakespeare, Ukraine Leader Vows No Surrender
2022-03-09.abstract: In a dramatic video address to Britain’s House of Commons, President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine said he would never capitulate to the invading Russians.

2022-03-09.title: Yoon Suk-yeol helped prosecute presidents. Now he wants to be one.
2022-03-09.abstract: Mr. Yoon, the leading conservative candidate, hopes to win the election by appealing to South Koreans who are deeply dissatisfied with the outgoing administration.

2022-03-09.title: Lee Jae-myung is a technocrat with hardscrabble origins.
2022-03-09.abstract: His challenge is to promise continuity with the ruling party’s policies, yet distinguish himself from the current president.

2022-03-09.title: Empty Stores and an Exodus: Hong Kong’s Covid Crackdown Stirs Panic
2022-03-09.abstract: A surge in cases has alarmed many residents. But the government’s mixed messaging about whether it will impose a lockdown has also been destabilizing.

2022-03-09.title: A man who bought a Pokémon card for $57,000 with Covid relief money is sentenced to prison.
2022-03-09.abstract: The forfeitures have included Lamborghinis, gold bars and luxury goods from Dior.

2022-03-10.title: Guatemalan Women Face Up to 10 Years in Prison Under New Abortion Bill
2022-03-10.abstract: The measure, which is expected to be signed into law by Guatemala’s conservative president within weeks, would impose the harshest punishments for abortion of almost any country in Latin America.

2022-03-10.title: Ontario will drop its indoor mask mandate weeks after trucker protests.
2022-03-10.abstract: Officials in Ontario said on Wednesday that the province was dropping its universal mask mandate, weeks after truckers paralyzed Ottawa during their protests over coronavirus restrictions.

2022-03-10.title: Here are the latest developments in the war on Ukraine.
2022-03-10.abstract: The top diplomats from Ukraine and Russia failed to reach an agreement on Thursday to calm the fighting in Ukraine or even to ease the worsening humanitarian crisis, dashing hopes for a swift end to a two-week-old conflict that has killed hundreds and displaced millions.

2022-03-10.title: Modi’s Party Wins the Biggest Prize in India’s State Elections
2022-03-10.abstract: Uttar Pradesh, India’s most populous state, is staying in the Bharatiya Janata Party’s hands, as the party maintains its electoral success despite mounting economic hardship.

2022-03-10.title: Australia Asks: How Far Is Too Far in Making China a Campaign Weapon?
2022-03-10.abstract: Australia’s conservative government has claimed, without evidence, that the political opposition would cozy up to Beijing. To many, it has crossed a red line.

2022-03-11.title: Russia Batters and Encircles Ukrainian Cities, as Diplomacy Falters
2022-03-11.abstract: The first meeting during the war of the two countries’ foreign ministers yielded no cease-fire. The Russian side signaled that its invasion would not stop until Ukraine surrendered.

2022-03-11.title: Requests for compassionate release filed by federal prisoners swelled during the pandemic, a study finds.
2022-03-11.abstract: The number of requests for compassionate release filed by federal prisoners skyrocketed during the pandemic, according to a report by the U.S. Sentencing Commission released on Thursday, with risks associated with contracting Covid-19 a critical factor.

2022-03-11.title: Climate Change’s Threat to the Glorious Redwood, Both Here and Afar
2022-03-11.abstract: In 1936, foresters planted California redwoods in Victoria. How long will they grow in peace?

2022-03-11.title: Russia-Ukraine talks come to nothing as bombardment of cities continues.
2022-03-11.abstract: Thousands of people fled through ‘humanitarian corridors’ as Vice President Kamala Harris said that Russia should be investigated for war crimes. U.S. officials warned that Putin’s threat to seize businesses would lead to more ‘economic pain.’

2022-03-11.title: Congress approves $13.6 billion in emergency aid for Ukraine.
2022-03-11.abstract: WASHINGTON — Congress passed a major spending bill on Thursday that includes $13.6 billion in emergency aid for Ukraine as it battles Russia’s invasion.

2022-03-12.title: Queen Elizabeth is skipping next week’s Commonwealth Day service.
2022-03-12.abstract: It is still less than a month since the British monarch tested positive for the coronavirus.

2022-03-12.title: China dismisses two mayors and Shanghai closes schools as an outbreak spreads further.
2022-03-12.abstract: BEIJING — Two mayors have been dismissed in northeastern China, and Shanghai has closed its school system and shifted to online instruction, as a coronavirus outbreak in mainland China gathers speed.

2022-03-12.title: India Accidentally Fires a Missile at Pakistan. Calm Ensues.
2022-03-12.abstract: Mistrust runs deep between the nuclear-armed neighbors, but Pakistan had a low-key response to what India called a “technical malfunction.” No casualties were reported.

2022-03-12.title: Chaos of War in Ukraine Could Fuel New Covid Surge, Doctors Say
2022-03-12.abstract: War has pushed Covid out of people’s minds while forcing them into crowded and unsanitary conditions that are fertile ground for the coronavirus, polio and other pathogens.

2022-03-12.title: Pandemic Fears Give Way to a Rush for Bomb Shelters
2022-03-12.abstract: Since Vladimir Putin’s invasion of Ukraine, European anxiety has shifted from Covid to nuclear annihilation. Bunkers, survival guides and iodine pills are flying off the shelves.

2022-03-13.title: Saudi Arabia Puts 81 to Death, Despite Promises to Curb Executions
2022-03-13.abstract: It was the kingdom’s largest mass execution in years. In many cases, the charges involved “not a drop of blood,” a rights activist said.

2022-03-13.title: South Korea exceeds 380,000 new cases in a day shortly after its presidential election.
2022-03-13.abstract: South Korea reported another record for daily new coronavirus cases over the weekend, reaching 383,665 known cases on Saturday, according to the government — a fresh reminder that cases have yet to peak during the current surge that is being fueled by the highly contagious Omicron variant. Public health experts had projected at the end of February that new cases would reach 350,000 a day by the end of March, and it was unclear whether predictions for a peak had been reset.

2022-03-13.title: U.K. vs. Oligarchs: ‘The Gloves Are Now Off’
2022-03-13.abstract: Russia’s war in Ukraine has finally led the British government to go after ultrawealthy Russians in London. But curbing the flood of corrupt money will require going after more than the big names.

2022-03-13.title: Missiles Fired From Iran Hit Near U.S. Consulate Site in Iraq
2022-03-13.abstract: No serious injuries were reported from the strike, outside the city of Erbil in the Kurdish region of Iraq, and an American official said the consulate site was undamaged.

2022-03-13.title: The base attacked in western Ukraine has been a hub for foreign militaries.
2022-03-13.abstract: The base outside Lviv that was attacked by Russian forces early on Sunday was a key link between Ukraine’s armed forces and Western militaries before the war.

2022-03-14.title: Foxconn, which assembles Apple’s iPhones, suspends production at factories in Shenzhen, China, because of a lockdown.
2022-03-14.abstract: The Taiwanese electronics firm said it “adjusted the production line” to other plants in China to minimize any impact on operations.

2022-03-14.title: China Sees at Least One Winner Emerging From Ukraine War: China
2022-03-14.abstract: The country’s leaders think it can shield itself from economic and diplomatic fallout and eventually be seen as a pillar of stability.

2022-03-14.title: Surge of Omicron Infections Prompts Lockdowns in China
2022-03-14.abstract: After two years of largely containing coronavirus outbreaks, China’s “Covid zero” policies are being tested by the highly transmissible variant.

2022-03-14.title: The New York Times launches a Telegram channel to deliver news about the war.
2022-03-14.abstract: The channel delivers reporting from our continuous live blog, where Times journalists are providing witness accounts, interviews and breaking news from the Russia-Ukraine conflict.

2022-03-14.title: A pregnant woman photographed after a Russian strike on a maternity hospital has died, The A.P. reports.
2022-03-14.abstract: Her baby also died, the news organization said. The photo captured last week has become one of the defining images of human suffering in the war.

2022-03-15.title: Zelensky calls on Russian troops to surrender.
2022-03-15.abstract: The Ukrainian leader and the Pentagon have both said some Russian soldiers have surrendered or been captured.

2022-03-15.title: Here are the latest developments in Ukraine.
2022-03-15.abstract: KYIV, Ukraine — Three European leaders were traveling to Kyiv on Tuesday to express the European Union’s “unequivocal support” for Ukraine and offer financial help, the Czech and Polish governments said, even as fighting rages around the city, with half a dozen loud blasts shaking central Kyiv in the pre-dawn hours. The leaders of the Czech Republic, Poland and Slovenia were traveling by train and crossed into Ukraine on Tuesday morning, officials said.

2022-03-15.title: These Foreign Fighters Are Joining the Battle for Ukraine
2022-03-15.abstract: Thousands of foreign volunteers — including Americans — are arriving in Ukraine to help defend the country. But an attack on their training camp on Sunday has left some wondering if they’ll ever see the front lines.

2022-03-15.title: Attack Hits Kyiv Neighborhood
2022-03-15.abstract: An explosion rocked a residential neighborhood in Kyiv, killing one and injuring six. It was the second attack on a civilian area in the Ukrainian capital on Monday.

2022-03-15.title: Indian Court Upholds Ban on Hijabs in Schools
2022-03-15.abstract: One school’s initial ban set off unrest and violence that spread to other schools in the southern state of Karnataka.

2022-03-16.title: In Reversal, Guatemala Shelves Anti-Abortion Law
2022-03-16.abstract: President Alejandro Giammattei said he would veto the bill days after his allies pushed it through the legislature, a move that surprised analysts, human rights activists and officials.

2022-03-16.title: North Korea’s Projectile Launch Fails, South Korea Says
2022-03-16.abstract: The projectile, fired from outside Pyongyang, failed “immediately after liftoff,” the South Korean military said.

2022-03-16.title: Zelensky urges more leaders to visit Kyiv after a visit by prime ministers of 3 NATO nations.
2022-03-16.abstract: The leaders from Poland, the Czech Republic and Slovenia “fear nothing,” President Volodymyr Zelensky said, and he urged other leaders to make similar visits.

2022-03-16.title: Oligarchs Got Richer Despite Sanctions. Will This Time Be Different?
2022-03-16.abstract: For nearly a decade, sanctions have been little more than names on a list for wealthy Russians. Governments are working to give them bite.

2022-03-16.title: Mass Graves Identified in Syria Could Hold Evidence of War Crimes
2022-03-16.abstract: Two sites have been located, believed to hold thousands of bodies of Syrians killed in detention centers administered by President Bashar al-Assad’s government during the civil war.

2022-03-17.title: Biden calls Putin a ‘war criminal,’ in his latest verbal escalation against Russia.
2022-03-17.abstract: The U.S. president’s unscripted remarks echoed comments other American officials have made recently.

2022-03-17.title: Sandstorm Blankets Cities Across Europe
2022-03-17.abstract: Sand from the Sahara drifted north into Europe, coating cars in thick reddish dust and turning skies orange in cities across the continent.

2022-03-17.title: A Russian Rapper Brings His Banned Antiwar Message to Istanbul
2022-03-17.abstract: The rapper, Oxxxymiron, said proceeds from his show would go to help Ukrainian refugees. Russians at the concert denounced the war but said they felt helpless to stop it.

2022-03-17.title: It Could’ve Been the World’s Largest Potato, if Only It Were a Potato
2022-03-17.abstract: A couple in New Zealand found a giant growth in their garden, named it Doug and applied to Guinness World Records. Then the results of a DNA analysis came in.

2022-03-17.title: Russia Is Destroying Kharkiv
2022-03-17.abstract: Residents describe what has been lost after three weeks of attacks.

2022-03-18.title: Kids, Covid Isolation and Learning to Endure
2022-03-18.abstract: The virus found us, and we’re doing just fine. I think.

2022-03-18.title: For a Ukrainian Poet, Putin’s War Is All Too Familiar
2022-03-18.abstract: Ihor Kalynets, 83, spent a lifetime resisting Soviet domination. Now, he says, he’s not going anywhere.

2022-03-18.title: Pope Deplores the War in Ukraine but Not the Aggressor
2022-03-18.abstract: Francis has raised his voice against “unacceptable armed aggression” without mentioning President Vladimir V. Putin or Russia. Some analysts say that he risks complicating his legacy.

2022-03-18.title: A ‘Killing Stone’ Broke in Japan. Is a Demon on the Loose?
2022-03-18.abstract: The rock appears in a famous legend starring a nine-tailed fox spirit. The question now is whether the fracture was a good or bad omen.

2022-03-18.title: War in Ukraine Prompts a Political Makeover in Poland
2022-03-18.abstract: Jaroslaw Kaczynski, Poland’s de facto leader and longtime scourge of E.U. unity, is portraying himself as a standard-bearer of European solidarity in defense of Ukraine and democratic values.

2022-03-19.title: Assad Visits U.A.E. in Latest Sign of Re-engagement
2022-03-19.abstract: It was the long-shunned Syrian leader’s first trip to an Arab country since his nation’s civil war erupted in 2011.

2022-03-19.title: Los Angeles schools will stop requiring masks in an agreement with the teachers’ union.
2022-03-19.abstract: The nation’s second-largest public school district, which had held out even after the state dropped its mandate, will continue to strongly recommend masks in classrooms.

2022-03-19.title: ‘They Keep Killing Us’: Violence Rages in Sudan’s Darfur Two Decades On
2022-03-19.abstract: The resurgence of ethnically motivated attacks in the country’s restive western region has led to enormous displacement and a growing humanitarian crisis.

2022-03-19.title: The Battle for Kyiv Looms as a Long and Bloody Conflict
2022-03-19.abstract: Ukraine’s capital is the biggest prize of all for the Russian military. If Russia tries to take control, it could lead to one of the biggest urban conflicts since World War II.

2022-03-19.title: Zelensky Pleads to Parliament for More Help. What Can Canada Offer?
2022-03-19.abstract: The address by President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine was also a reminder that Canada might indirectly benefit from the crisis.

2022-03-20.title: Its offensive slowed, Russia uses long-range missiles to devastating effect.
2022-03-20.abstract: Even as the Russian ground advance on key targets including Kyiv and Odessa remains stalled, it has used long-range rockets in recent days to devastating effect against the Ukrainian military and infrastructure.

2022-03-20.title: Here are the latest developments in the war in Ukraine.
2022-03-20.abstract: Russian forces made significant gains in Ukraine, advancing into the port of Mariupol, destroying an underground weapons depot and leaving a barracks in ruins following one of the deadliest rocket strikes on Ukraine’s military in the nearly month-old war.

2022-03-20.title: Hillsong Church Says Brian Houston, Its Founder, Breached Code of Conduct
2022-03-20.abstract: The board of the global megachurch, which got its start in Australia, apologized “unreservedly” to two women who had accused Mr. Houston of inappropriate behavior.

2022-03-20.title: ‘I Don’t Have the Right to Cry’: Ukrainian Women Share Their Stories of Escape
2022-03-20.abstract: In war, things can change quickly. A New York Times reporter wanted to capture — in whatever blurry way one can in a war — this moment in this war, three weeks in.

2022-03-20.title: Russian Soldiers Took Their City, Then Their Homes
2022-03-20.abstract: As Russian forces pushed toward Kyiv, they stormed an apartment complex in a nearby suburb and held residents hostage. Some of it was caught on camera.

2022-03-21.title: Disney Shanghai temporarily shuts down as Omicron surges in China.
2022-03-21.abstract: The move came amid growing restrictions on travel and entertainment across China as the government tries to stamp out the Omicron variant.

2022-03-21.title: Hong Kong will lift its bans on flights and cut quarantine times.
2022-03-21.abstract: Carrie Lam, the chief executive, announced the measures on Monday, as experts said the worst of the latest wave might have passed.

2022-03-21.title: These Revered Cranes Escaped Extinction. Can They Survive Without Humans?
2022-03-21.abstract: Conservators vastly increased the numbers of red-crowned cranes, a symbol of loyalty and longevity in Japanese culture. That’s just a start.

2022-03-21.title: An Ancient City Transformed by War
2022-03-21.abstract: Simple rituals, like having coffee in a cafe in Lviv, Ukraine, take on a new and sometimes surreal meaning in a radically altered context.

2022-03-21.title: Shares of Boeing are lower after the China Eastern plane crash.
2022-03-21.abstract: The aerospace giant said it was seeking more information on the disaster, and U.S. regulators said they were ready to assist.

2022-03-22.title: On the Scene in Poland
2022-03-22.abstract: A sprawling expo center in the Polish capital has given some 25,000 refugees a safe place to recharge before journeying on.

2022-03-22.title: In Warsaw, a Network of Support Greets Those Crossing the Polish Border
2022-03-22.abstract: A sprawling expo center in the Polish capital has given some 25,000 refugees a safe place to recharge before journeying on.

2022-03-22.title: Zelensky condemns Russian attacks on protesters and children.
2022-03-22.abstract: ‘It was a day of difficult events,’ the Ukrainian president said in a video address.

2022-03-22.title: Gurkha Veterans Fight a Colonial-Era Legacy Still Shaping Nepal
2022-03-22.abstract: Nepal’s best and brightest have been recruited while young to fight for Britain for over 200 years, with their pay and pensions a fraction of their fellow British soldiers’ for much of that time.

2022-03-22.title: No Survivors Found in China Eastern Crash, Officials Say
2022-03-22.abstract: Workers recovered identity cards, cellphones and purses from the crash site. But the plane’s steep plunge suggested there was a “minuscule” chance of any survivors, an expert said.

2022-03-23.title: South Africa begins to lift Covid restrictions but leaves its indoor mask mandate.
2022-03-23.abstract: South Africa will begin lifting Covid-19 restrictions on Wednesday, President Cyril Ramaphosa said on Tuesday in an address that marked the start of what he called a “new era” in the country’s fight against the pandemic.

2022-03-23.title: Here are the latest developments.
2022-03-23.abstract: President Biden is heading to Brussels on Wednesday to meet with NATO allies and is expected to announce new sanctions aimed at Russian lawmakers as the United States and its allies aim to pressure Russia from all sides. His trip comes as Ukrainian forces have mounted a counteroffensive that they say has retaken ground outside Kyiv.

2022-03-23.title: Founder of Australia’s Hillsong Church Resigns Amid Scandals
2022-03-23.abstract: Brian Houston was accused of breaching the church’s code of conduct after two women said he had behaved inappropriately.

2022-03-23.title: Russia’s Attacks on Civilian Targets Have Obliterated Everyday Life in Ukraine
2022-03-23.abstract: In the weeks since Russia began its invasion, more than 1,500 civilian buildings, structures and vehicles in Ukraine have been damaged or destroyed.

2022-03-23.title: A Town on Ukraine’s Edge, Determined to Escape Its Past
2022-03-23.abstract: Przemysl’s history has been intertwined with war. This time, like much of Poland, it wants to do things differently.

2022-03-24.title: Another ‘One-in-500-Year’ Flood, and a Government Not Up to the Task
2022-03-24.abstract: Civilians were largely left to rescue one another as a natural disaster struck Australia. It shows how the authorities are struggling to respond to climate-fueled calamities.

2022-03-24.title: With U.S. Focus on Ukraine, North Korea Launches a Powerful New ICBM
2022-03-24.abstract: It was the country’s first long-range ballistic missile test since 2017, and it raised the specter of intensified provocations and threats on the Korean Peninsula.

2022-03-24.title: Fed Up With Deadly Propaganda, Some Russian Journalists Quit
2022-03-24.abstract: At least four state television employees have publicly resigned, citing regret for their roles in promoting false narratives about Ukraine.

2022-03-24.title: In Reversal, Australia Agrees to Send Offshore Refugees to New Zealand
2022-03-24.abstract: Under the arrangement, first offered in 2013, New Zealand will take in 150 refugees a year for three years from Australia’s widely criticized detention system.

2022-03-24.title: ‘Is There Shooting Here?’ Ukrainians Find Refuge in Moldova
2022-03-24.abstract: Moldova, one of Europe’s smallest and poorest countries, has taken in more than 100,000 refugees from Ukraine. But Moldova’s ability to protect them is reaching a breaking point.

2022-03-25.title: China and Solomon Islands Draft Secret Security Pact, Raising Alarm in the Pacific
2022-03-25.abstract: The leaked agreement, if signed, could help the Chinese Navy block shipping routes that played a vital role in World War II.

2022-03-25.title: Recurring Floods, and Other Disasters, Take Their Toll
‘Can’t Cope’: Australia’s Great Barrier Reef Suffers 6th Mass Bleaching Event
2022-03-25.abstract: This year offers a disturbing first: mass bleaching in a year of La Niña. The grim milestone points to the continued threat of climate change and greenhouse gas emissions.

2022-03-25.title: ‘I’m Old, Not an Idiot.’ One Man’s Protest Gets Attention of Spanish Banks
2022-03-25.abstract: A campaign by a 78-year-old retired doctor with Parkinson’s disease against banks closing branches and moving services online resonates with a rapidly aging Spanish population.

2022-03-25.title: Sri Lanka Economy Has ‘Hit Rock Bottom,’ Putting Pressure on President
2022-03-25.abstract: A debt crisis is disrupting life across Sri Lanka, where food and fuel are suddenly either unavailable or exorbitantly priced. Protests are rising against a president with a reputation for brutality.

2022-03-26.title: The Biden administration plans to offer a second booster shot to older Americans.
2022-03-26.abstract: WASHINGTON — The Biden administration is planning to give Americans age 50 or older the option of a second booster of the Pfizer-BioNTech or Moderna coronavirus vaccine without recommending outright that they get one, according to several people familiar with the plan.

2022-03-26.title: New York’s mayor faces a backlash over a vaccine exemption for athletes.
2022-03-26.abstract: Since lifting the private employer vaccine requirement for professional athletes and performers based in New York City on Thursday, Mayor Eric Adams has drawn the ire of city employees, their unions and others who still must adhere to the mandate or lose their jobs.

2022-03-26.title: A Desperate Escape From Mariupol
2022-03-26.abstract: Ukrainian families are taking enormous risks to try to flee Mariupol, traveling through Russian checkpoints — and in some cases land mines — to try to get to safety.

2022-03-26.title: A Minnesota man is released after being held by Russian troops for 10 days.
2022-03-26.abstract: Tyler Jacob was taken off a bus by Russian soldiers at a checkpoint in Crimea, his mother said. He had been teaching English in the Ukrainian city of Kherson.

2022-03-26.title: Mideast Feels the Pinch of Rising Food Prices as Ramadan Nears
2022-03-26.abstract: Russia’s war on Ukraine has driven up the prices of staple foods and energy across the Middle East and North Africa ahead of the Muslim holy month of daytime fasting and nighttime feasting.

2022-03-27.title: Zelensky renews his call for planes and tanks from NATO.
2022-03-27.abstract: President Volodymyr Zelensky said European leaders were falling short in helping his nation fight Russia and urged them to show even a bit of the courage that the outmanned residents of Mariupol have demonstrated.

2022-03-27.title: Second Flight Recorder From China Crash Is Found, Officials Say
2022-03-27.abstract: The cause of the crash of China Eastern Flight 5735, which killed 132 people on Monday, remains unknown.

2022-03-27.title: Amid War, a Lavish Hotel Will Open in Britain’s Old War Office
2022-03-27.abstract: An extravagant conversion is nearly complete on the Old War Office, an Edwardian-era monument to Britain’s imperial past being made into a five-star hotel and high-end apartments.

2022-03-27.title: Blinken to Press Mideast Allies for Stronger Support for Ukraine
2022-03-27.abstract: The countries, which have their own relationships with Russia, have provided some modest backing, but Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken will ask for more.

2022-03-27.title: French Far-Right Candidate Vows to Fight for Identity as Prospects Fade
2022-03-27.abstract: Éric Zemmour, a far-right pundit, tried to revive his flagging campaign Sunday in a place that is familiar to hopefuls of his ilk.

2022-03-28.title: Explosion of Gang Violence Grips El Salvador, Setting Record
2022-03-28.abstract: Gangs went on a killing spree, gunning down anyone on the street. At least 62 were killed on Saturday, the deadliest day on record since the country’s civil war ended 30 years ago.

2022-03-28.title: General Strike Throws India Into Confusion
2022-03-28.abstract: The two-day strike, involving both public and private sector workers, was called to protest the Modi government’s economic policies, including a privatization plan.

2022-03-28.title: Pope Meets With Groups Requesting Apology for Indigenous Schools
2022-03-28.abstract: For the first time, Francis received Indigenous survivors of abusive boarding schools in Canada who were seeking the pontiff’s apology over the church’s role in running the schools.

2022-03-28.title: ‘Drive My Car’ Oscar Is a Slow-Burn Return for Japan’s Cinema
2022-03-28.abstract: But some of the same qualities that made the film popular overseas have led to a cooler reception domestically.

2022-03-28.title: Canadian Indigenous Groups Seeking Apology Meet With Pope
2022-03-28.abstract: Pope Francis met with Métis and Inuit delegates on the first day of meetings aimed at persuading the pope to apologize for the church’s role in running schools that abused Indigenous children for over 100 years.

2022-03-29.title: Coronavirus deaths in the U.S. fall to their lowest point since the summer.
2022-03-29.abstract: Fewer than 800 coronavirus deaths are being reported each day in the United States, the lowest daily average since before the Omicron variant took hold late last fall. The last time the rate was this low was in mid-August, according to a New York Times database.

2022-03-29.title: A Brutal Russian Playbook Reapplied in Ukraine
2022-03-29.abstract: The besieging of cities, the deliberate targeting of civilians — they are the tactics Russia used before, in Grozny. I saw it firsthand.

2022-03-29.title: Solomon Islands’ Leader Calls Concern Over China Security Deal ‘Insulting’
2022-03-29.abstract: Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare criticized Australia and New Zealand as assuming that the island nation could not act in its own best interests.

2022-03-29.title: London Police Issue Fines for Downing Street Lockdown Parties
2022-03-29.abstract: Prime Minister Boris Johnson was not among those penalized, his office said, but the police decision puts a perilous political scandal back in the spotlight.

2022-03-29.title: Shanghai’s Lockdown Tests Covid-Zero Policy, and People’s Limits
2022-03-29.abstract: A staggered lockdown on China’s commercial capital of 26 million people has sent beleaguered residents scrambling and raised fears of broader damage to the economy.

2022-03-30.title: In Life, They Showed Israel’s Breadth. In Death, They Were Victims.
2022-03-30.abstract: The five victims of Israel’s deadliest terrorist attack in years came from different walks of life, highlighting the complexity of Israeli society.

2022-03-30.title: Putin’s War Is Complicating India’s Middle Path Among Powers
2022-03-30.abstract: India has been reluctant to criticize Russia, long an important ally. But China’s rise, and its closeness to Vladimir Putin, is creating new pressure.

2022-03-30.title: Driven From City Life to Jungle Insurgency
2022-03-30.abstract: Fleeing the military’s brutal crackdown, Myanmar’s newest rebels have abandoned cafes and professions to join a near-daily battle with long odds.

2022-03-30.title: Family’s Balcony Death in Switzerland Appears to Be Suicide, Police Say
2022-03-30.abstract: The investigation suggests that the victims jumped from the seventh-floor balcony “one after the other,” the police said.

2022-03-30.title: A Mural Lionizing an Indian Ruler Was Sold at Auction. His Legacy Is Contested.
2022-03-30.abstract: The battle scene depicts a victory over British troops by an 18th-century Muslim, a man India’s current government would prefer was forgotten.

2022-03-31.title: In Hong Kong, China’s Covid Aid Gets the Cold Shoulder
2022-03-31.abstract: Disputes about medics, squat toilets and diaper-changing duties underscore longstanding tensions between residents of Hong Kong and the mainland.

2022-03-31.title: What is the Wagner Group?
2022-03-31.abstract: The private Russian military force, which has close ties to President Vladimir Putin and has been used in war zones across the world, is expanding its presence in Ukraine.

2022-03-31.title: Australian Journalist Who Worked for Chinese Media Stands Trial in Beijing
2022-03-31.abstract: Cheng Lei, a former CGTN host, was detained in 2020. The Chinese authorities have accused her of divulging state secrets but offered no specifics.

2022-03-31.title: A Nation of Spy-Catchers: Fear of Saboteurs Has Ukrainians on Edge
2022-03-31.abstract: There are checkpoints, hotlines and apps for reporting suspicious activities. Ukrainians worry about saboteurs mixing among them sowing mistrust and spreading misinformation.

2022-03-31.title: North Korean ICBM Launch May Have Been Fake. It’s Still Important.
2022-03-31.abstract: South Korea says Kim Jong-un disguised an older missile as a new one to exaggerate his country’s weapons achievements when he badly needs political leverage.
